disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Multiple long-standing (about 6 months) rough, hard, fleshy-greyish raised bumps on the dorsal fingers with a cauliflower-like surface texture, previously entirely painless.
trigger_question_examples: How long have these bumps been on his fingers?
What do the bumps look and feel like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's had these rough hard bumps on his fingers for about six months. They're fleshy-greyish and rough on top, and they've never hurt or bled until today.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute traumatic partial avulsion of the largest lesion after it was snagged on a door frame, exposing a highly vascular base with sudden continuous point bleeding disproportionate to a simple graze.
trigger_question_examples: What happened just before the bleeding started?
How much is it bleeding compared with a normal small cut?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: About an hour ago he caught the biggest bump on a door frame and the top tore off. It started bleeding straight away and it's bled a lot more than a normal little cut would.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Tiny dark specks (thrombosed capillaries / 'black dots') at the base of the torn lesion and loss of the normal skin-line pattern across the bump — features of a viral wart's vascular core.
trigger_question_examples: Can you see anything unusual at the base of the bump — any dark dots?
Do the normal fingerprint lines carry across the bump or stop at it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There are little black dots in the raw part at the base, and the normal fingerprint lines don't carry across the bump — they stop at its edge.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Several similar smaller lesions on the adjacent finger with a history of scratching/picking — a pattern of local spread (autoinoculation) rather than a solitary lesion.
trigger_question_examples: Is it just the one bump or are there others?
Does he pick or scratch at them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's not just one — there are a few smaller ones on the next finger, and he does pick and scratch at them, and new little ones keep appearing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No systemic or local infective features — afebrile, child systemically well, and no surrounding cellulitic erythema, induration, warmth, purulence or ascending red streaks around the lesion.
trigger_question_examples: Does he have a fever or seem unwell in himself?
Is the skin around the bump red, hot, swollen or leaking pus, and are there any red streaks up his hand or arm?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever and he's not unwell in himself. The skin around it isn't red, hot, puffy or leaking pus, and there are no red streaks going up his hand or arm.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The child is haemodynamically well to the parent's eye (alert, not pale, sweaty, floppy or drowsy) and there is no personal or family history of a bleeding or bruising tendency; blood loss is modest and slows with pressure.
trigger_question_examples: Is he pale, drowsy, floppy or sweaty at all?
Does he bruise or bleed very easily normally, or does anyone in the family?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's alert and his colour is normal — not pale or floppy. He doesn't bruise or bleed easily normally and no one in the family does either. When we press firmly it slows right down.
